title: NOC 21390 – Aerospace Engineers | GTR Immigration
description: Aerospace Engineers are in rising demand across Canada as aircraft manufacturing expands, satellite programs grow, and defence, research, and space-technology investments accelerate. Classified under NOC 21390 (TEER 1), this occupation is well suited for:
duties: Design aircraft, spacecraft, satellites, and missile systems
Run advanced computer simulations and modelling
Specify aerospace materials and manufacturing processes
Supervise assembly, modification, and overhaul operations
Prepare maintenance manuals and operational standards
Investigate accidents or structural failures and recommend fixes
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in aerospace or related engineering field
job_outlook: This signals competitive but accessible prospects—especially for candidates with:
pathways: Mobility into drone, space, and AI-aided flight systems
additional_information: Canada’s aerospace ecosystem—spanning aircraft manufacturing, avionics, satellites, drones, and defence systems—continues to recruit engineers for:
Aircraft & spacecraft designStructural analysis and testingSatellite systemsFlight-test engineeringAdvanced materialsSpace-communications hardware
Annual salaries often range from $51,800–$146,100 CAD, depending on province, clearance level, specialization, and licensing status.
